name: BTC Session Drive Continuation v1
edge_mechanism: opening-range or session-window continuation when a specific session structurally dominates follow-through
thesis: BTC 1h is the best lane for testing whether one session's directional drive creates a continuation edge into the next bar cluster once price holds above fast value. The edge is the persistent session drive after initial acceptance, not the first impulse itself.
invalidation_condition: Abort if session-drive holds remain too sparse, or if held drives repeatedly mean-revert before exits can cover costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A session produces a directional drive, price holds that drive rather than fully mean-reverting, and continuation follows for a short second leg.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: When a particular session dominates order flow, subsequent bars often continue the move if the drive survives the first retrace.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: BTC 1h is the cleanest allowed lane for session-structure logic because it expresses intraday persistence better than 4h while staying liquid.

indicator_role_map.EMA_20: fast hold reference after session drive

pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: Session drives may look real initially but fade back into chop too quickly, leaving insufficient continuation distance.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries are dense enough but travel is weak, simplify exits before tightening the hold condition.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A workable set of continuation trades with the first profit layer carrying most expectancy and a smaller runner contribution.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: BTC 1h plus one cross-lane show credible density and positive post-cost continuation after drive holds.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sparse mirage, or repeated fast fade after the session drive.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; drive, hold, and directional participation are complementary
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: improved by using broad hold-after-drive grammar rather than exact session timestamp triggers

validation_basket.why_included: Best lane for intraday session-drive continuation inside the allowed basket.
validation_basket.continue_if: Held session drives trade often enough and continue after costs.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: Drive holds are sparse or fade immediately.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Persistent session moves that survive first retrace and continue for a second leg.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same hold-after-drive logic generalizes to a slower major lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH preserves continuation behavior with acceptable density.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The pattern only exists on BTC intraday structure.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Fewer but cleaner drive continuations than BTC 1h.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the same continuation grammar survives on the adjacent slower BTC lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: 4h still shows hold-and-go behavior without collapsing trade count.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Less frequent but larger continuation legs.
